{{Infobox book
| name = Dreadgod
| slug = cradle-11-dreadgod
| precedes = [[Waybound (novel)|Waybound]]
| follows = [[Reaper (novel)|Reaper]]
| cover = [[File:Cover-cradle-11-dreadgod.jpg|250px]]
| released = 2022
| isbn = 9781959001089
| publisher = Hidden Gnome Publishing
| pages = 484
}}

== Plot summary ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The story begins at the [[Hall of Judgment]] on [[Sanctum]], where the [[Judges]] sentence Ozriel to be leashed as a controlled asset following his perceived desertion. As tensions rise across the cosmos, [[Lindon]] works to repair [[Dross]]'s internal structure using an echo of [[Ozmanthus Arelius]], only to receive a global emergency broadcast from [[Emriss Silentborn]]: all four [[Dreadgods]] have awakened. Amidst this chaos, the [[Silent King]] begins exerting parasitic control over various cities and minds. [[Lindon]] prepares for war by using his new hammer, [[Genesis]], to craft protective constructs for his allies, while [[Yerin]] seeks to increase her power by addressing a [[hunger madra]] deficiency. 

As the conflict escalates, [[Lindon]] and [[Mercy]] conspire to obtain the schematics for the The Book of Eternal Night{{!}}Book of Eternal Night, while [[Orthos]] and [[Ziel]] navigate ancient ruins to uncover the tragic history of the Black dragons{{!}}black dragon [[Noroloth]]. In [[Redmoon Hall]], [[Yerin]] forms an alliance with the [[Sage of Red Faith]] to overthrow Redmoon Herald{{!}}Herald Redmoon, eventually achieving [[Archlord]] advancement. The situation turns dire when the [[Wandering Titan]] attacks [[Moongrave]], and the [[Silent King]] manifests globally, using [[dream aura]] to reshape reality. [[Lindon]] engages in a high-stakes mental confrontation with the [[Dreadgod]], eventually utilizing his [[Void Icon]] to consume stolen energy and execute a coordinated strike alongside [[Monarchs]] [[Northstrider]] and [[Emriss Silentborn]]. This massive power transformation allows [[Lindon]] to wound the [[Silent King]] and escape, though he is subsequently branded as a corrupt entity known as the '[[Empty Ghost]]' by [[Akura Malice]].

In the aftermath, [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] use the [[Void Icon]] to hide from [[Fate]] as they embark on a series of high-stakes thefts from [[Monarch]]-controlled facilities. They use the [[Dreamway]] to broadcast a message exposing the [[Monarchs]]' complicity in the [[Dreadgod]] crisis and announcing [[Lindon]] as the killer of the [[Silent King]]. Following a violent confrontation with [[Akura Malice]] to rescue [[Mercy]], [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] retreat to prepare for the next threat. Meanwhile, in the higher realms, [[the Mad King]] [[Daruman]] discovers a potential path to kill a [[Judge]], and the [[Judges]] Ozriel and [[Suriel]] prepare for a likely fatal confrontation with him as the world faces an era of unprecedented instability.}}

== Chapter-by-chapter ==
* '''Chapter 1:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Judges]] convene at the [[Hall of Judgment]] on [[Sanctum]] to sentence Ozriel, who has been brought back in a cage and silenced by a powerful metal band. After the gag is removed, Ozriel engages in a heated verbal confrontation with the other [[Judges]], specifically [[Makiel]] and [[Gadrael]], regarding his perceived desertion and the devastation caused by a stolen weapon. Ultimately, the [[Court]] decides to strip Ozriel of his Authority{{!}}authority and leash him as a controlled asset, [[binding]] him with powerful iron-and-crystal manacles{{!}}manacles to assist in addressing an escalating crisis in [[Sector Seven]].}}
* '''Chapter 2:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|After searching [[Eithan]]'s empty hut, [[Lindon]] enters labyrinth{{!}}the labyrinth to use a [[Forger binding]] to summon an echo of [[Ozmanthus Arelius]] for guidance. With the help of the echo, [[Lindon]] successfully repairs [[Dross]]'s internal structure, making the spirit more efficient than before. The chapter concludes as [[Lindon]] and [[Dross]] receive a global emergency broadcast from [[Emriss Silentborn]], announcing that all four [[Dreadgods]] have awakened and prompting [[Lindon]] to prepare for battle.}}
* '''Chapter 3:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|After [[Kerani]] discovers her supervisor is under the control of the [[Silent King]], she is rescued by a dark spirit named [[Dross]] and witnesses the [[Dreadgod]]'s influence over the city. Meanwhile, [[Yerin]] battles corrupted leviathans alongside members of the The Eight-Man Empire{{!}}Eight-Man Empire, and [[Lindon]] engages in a high-stakes mental confrontation with the [[Silent King]]. Following these encounters, [[Lindon]] meets with [[Akura Pride]] to discuss the growing threat and begins utilizing labyrinth{{!}}the labyrinth's resources to prepare his allies for the coming war.}}
* '''Chapter 4:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Lindon]] uses the [[Soulforge]] and his new hammer, [[Genesis]], to craft six protective clamshell constructs designed to resist the [[Silent King]]'s parasitic control for his family and allies. After completing the task, [[Lindon]] confronts the [[Monarch]] [[Northstrider]], who refuses to leave the world due to his refusal to bow to the [[Abidan]]. Meanwhile, [[Northstrider]] joins [[Sha Miara]] to defend [[Ninecloud City]] against the [[Weeping Dragon]], only to be joined by [[Reigan Shen]] and [[House Shen]], who have shifted their priorities toward addressing a new global threat.}}
* '''Chapter 5:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Lindon]] and his companions, including [[Yerin]], [[Ziel]], [[Orthos]], and [[Little Blue]], discuss their future plans to advance their power and prepare for potential battles against [[Dreadgods]]. [[Lindon]] provides the group with protective constructs to defend against threats from the [[Silent King]] and outlines specific paths for each member to grow stronger. Afterward, [[Lindon]] travels to [[Serpent's Grave]] to provide his sister [[Kelsa]] with protection and confronts [[Jai Long]] regarding his past actions and character.}}
* '''Chapter 6:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Lindon]] and [[Ziel]] navigate the ancient, shifting [[labyrinth]] of the Ancestor's Tomb{{!}}Ancestor’s Tomb to retrieve a [[hunger madra]] spear and study primordial runes. Meanwhile, in [[Moongrave]], [[Malice]] and her allies engage the [[Wandering Titan]] in a devastating battle that forces a mass evacuation through a Shadow{{!}}shadow portal. Simultaneously, the [[Judges]] Ozriel and [[Suriel]] strike back against the [[Silverlords]] by sabotaging their operations across multiple [[Iterations]] to disrupt [[the Mad King]]'s influence.}}
* '''Chapter 7:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|After [[Yerin]] enters [[Redmoon Hall]] to cooperate with the [[Blood Sage]], [[Lindon]] departs from her and leads [[Orthos]] and [[Ziel]] toward [[Shatterspine Castle]]. Upon arriving at the ancient, time-locked fortress, [[Lindon]] uses his abilities to break the seal and harvest residual [[force madra]]. The group is then approached by a shadowy vulture messenger representing the [[Beast King]], who acknowledges their arrival and the importance of their mission.}}
* '''Chapter 8:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|As the [[Wandering Titan]] marches toward [[Moongrave]], [[Mercy]] manages clan affairs and navigates growing public anxiety regarding the awakening [[Dreadgods]]. After receiving news of the [[Titan]]'s course, [[Mercy]] is confronted by her mother, [[Malice]], who expresses suspicion regarding [[Lindon]] and [[Eithan]]'s true intentions. [[Lindon]] arrives at the city gates alone, where he is subjected to a series of tests and a forced tour by [[Akura Justice]] before finally meeting [[Mercy]] in private to propose a plan to rob her mother.}}
* '''Chapter 9:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Yerin]] navigates the political tension between Redmoon Herald{{!}}Herald Redmoon and the [[Sage of Red Faith]] within [[Redmoon Hall]], eventually cooperating with the [[Sage]] to undergo memory extraction and blood analysis. The [[Sage]] reveals that [[Yerin]] has an aspect deficiency in [[hunger madra]], prompting her to seek out more power through combat. To facilitate her growth and tip the [[Hall]]'s internal balance, [[Yerin]] challenges and defeats an Overlord Emissaries{{!}}Emissary named [[Alden Zaius]], successfully absorbing a portion of his [[Blood Shadow]].}}
* '''Chapter 10:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Lindon]] and [[Mercy]] conspire to obtain the schematics for her The Book of Eternal Night{{!}}Book of Eternal Night, a plan that requires navigating the security and family politics of the [[Akura clan]]. Meanwhile, [[Orthos]] and [[Ziel]] explore a ruined castle where they discover a dream tablets{{!}}dream tablet revealing the tragic, corrupted history of the Black dragons{{!}}black dragon [[Noroloth]]. In another realm, the [[Judges]] [[Suriel]] and Ozriel discuss the necessity of allowing a doomed world to follow its destined path toward destruction.}}
* '''Chapter 11:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Sage of Red Faith]] realizes through [[Yerin]]'s memories that his failure to create a subordinate [[Blood Shadow]] was due to his own inability to cooperate with others. This realization leads to a violent confrontation with his [[Blood Shadow]], [[Redmoon]], who attempts to usurp him and seize control of the sect. After [[Yerin]] intervenes to save the [[Sage]], the two form an alliance to overthrow [[Redmoon Hall]] and address the existential threat posed by the [[Monarchs]] and [[Dreadgods]].}}
* '''Chapter 12:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|After [[Lindon]] accidentally violates an oath by implying he knows the connection between [[Monarchs]] and [[Dreadgods]], [[Charity]] confronts him and subsequently informs [[Malice]] of his transgression. [[Malice]] reveals the truth to [[Charity]]—that [[Dreadgods]] are the price paid for [[Monarchs]] to remain in [[Cradle]]—and eventually restores [[Lindon]]'s spirit after he suffers a near-fatal spiritual collapse. While [[Mercy]] searches frantically for the missing [[Lindon]], [[Charity]] directs her to his location while maintaining the secrecy of the oath, leaving [[Lindon]] to recover and prepare for the upcoming battle.}}
* '''Chapter 13:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|After a fierce battle with the [[Archlord]] [[dragon]] [[Helethshan]], [[Orthos]] and [[Ziel]] realize the [[dragon]] has been imprisoned for centuries and is unaware of the current empire. [[Helethshan]] leads them to a sealed room to retrieve a [[Remnant]], but instead, they are surrounded by a group of hostile Black dragons{{!}}black dragons. To escape the ensuing confrontation and the collapsing castle, [[Ziel]] uses his knowledge of runes to manipulate ancient [[scripts]] and leads [[Orthos]] into a reinforced room just as the ceiling falls.}}
* '''Chapter 14:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Yerin]] forms a desperate soul-oath{{!}}soul oath with the [[Sage of Red Faith]] and [[Archlady]] [[Kahn Mala]] to launch an ambush against the Redmoon Herald{{!}}Herald Redmoon. During the chaotic battle on the cloud fortress, the [[Bleeding Phoenix]] exerts a crushing influence that weakens everyone connected to its power. [[Yerin]] and her spirit, [[Little Blue]], use the hunger spears{{!}}hunger spear to channel [[Redmoon]]'s own power against him, ultimately allowing [[Little Blue]] to absorb and stabilize the energy to secure a victory.}}
* '''Chapter 15:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|As the [[Wandering Titan]] attacks [[Moongrave]], [[Malice]] and the [[Akura clan]]'s defenders engage in a devastating battle that forces [[Lindon]] to attempt repairing spatial cracks caused by the [[Dreadgod]]. The [[Titan]] uses a trade mechanism to summon another entity, resulting in the [[Silent King]] manifesting across the world and seizing control of numerous minds through [[dream aura]]. This global intrusion affects various characters, including [[Yerin]], [[Orthos]], and the [[Twin Star Sect]], as the [[Silent King]]'s influence begins to reshape reality.}}
* '''Chapter 16:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|As the [[Silent King]] uses powerful dream techniques to control the inhabitants of [[Moongrave]], [[Lindon]] and [[Charity]] work to free various [[Sages]] from mental domination. [[Lindon]] utilizes his ability to [[Consume]] the King's halos, rapidly increasing his power while intervening in several battles between [[Sages]] and [[Heralds]]. Realizing that the [[Silent King]]'s endless waves of mental control are causing mass chaos, [[Lindon]] decides he must stop freeing individuals and instead move to assist [[Malice]] in attacking the [[Dreadgod]] directly.}}
* '''Chapter 17:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|On the deck of [[Redmoon Hall]], [[Yerin]] fights alongside the [[Blood Sage]] to combat the Redmoon Herald{{!}}Herald Redmoon and a monstrous [[Remnant]] of the [[Bleeding Phoenix]]. After [[Yerin]] achieves an [[Archlord]] advancement, she defeats the phoenix avatar and strikes a deal to recruit the [[Blood Sage]]'s [[Remnant]] into her [[void key]]. Meanwhile, in [[Serpent's Grave]], [[Kelsa]] and [[Jai Chen]] survive an [[Underlord]]-level assault through [[Lindon]]'s automated countermeasures, while [[Lindon]]'s parents are rescued by the golden [[Remnant]] [[Ekeri]].}}
* '''Chapter 18:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|During a desperate battle against the [[Silent King]], [[Lindon]] enters a mental illusion where he resists the [[Dreadgod]]'s attempts to recruit him and instead uses his [[Void Icon]] to consume stolen energy. With the arrival of [[Monarchs]] [[Northstrider]] and [[Emriss Silentborn]], [[Lindon]] executes a coordinated strike that severely wounds the [[Dreadgod]]. Although the [[Silent King]] survives and attempts to call for reinforcements, [[Lindon]]'s unique abilities allow him to disrupt the connection, leaving the [[Dreadgod]] weakened and retreating.}}
* '''Chapter 19:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|After [[Lindon]] consumes the [[Silent King]] and undergoes a massive power transformation, he escapes from the furious [[Monarchs]] [[Northstrider]] and [[Malice]]. Meanwhile, [[Ziel]] and [[Orthos]] are rescued by a transformed [[Lindon]] from the wreckage of [[Shatterspine Castle]]. Finally, [[Lindon]] and a returned [[Dross]] arrive to defend [[Yerin]] from an attack by [[Reigan Shen]] and the [[Remnant]] of [[Tiberian Arelius]].}}
* '''Chapter 20:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Daruman]], [[the Mad King]], resides in his fortress [[Tal’gullour]], where he uses his powers to manipulate destiny and envision potential futures. Through these visions, he discovers that the [[Abidan]] [[Judge]] [[Makiel]] is willing to risk the life of [[Suriel]] The Phoenix{{!}}the Phoenix to protect his own position from trial. Realizing he finally has a path to kill a [[Judge]], [[Daruman]] orders his servitors to prepare for a new war.}}
* '''Chapter 21:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Following the chaotic battle against the [[Silent King]], [[Malice]] reveals her true nature to [[Mercy]] and removes the locks on her The Book of Eternal Night{{!}}Book of Eternal Night. Meanwhile, [[Lindon]] successfully evacuates his allies to the [[Sacred Valley]] just before [[Reigan Shen]] attacks and destroys [[Serpent's Grave]]. To prepare for the inevitable arrival of more [[Dreadgods]], [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] form a plan to steal powerful assets from various [[Monarchs]] to bolster their strength.}}
* '''Chapter 22:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] use the [[Void Icon]] to hide their movements from [[Fate]] as they embark on a series of thefts targeting [[Monarch]]-controlled facilities, including a preservation lab and the [[White Lion Bank]]. After successfully stealing various treasures, they infiltrate the [[Dreamway]] to broadcast a message through [[Emriss Silentborn]]'s network. [[Yerin]] uses the platform to expose the [[Monarchs]]' complicity in the [[Dreadgod]] crisis and announce [[Lindon]] as the killer of the [[Silent King]], sparking widespread shock and unrest.}}
* '''Chapter 23:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|After [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] finish crafting a time-locked pocket world, they confront the [[Monarch]] [[Akura Malice]] to rescue [[Mercy]]. A violent battle ensues between [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] against [[Malice]], the [[Sage]] [[Charity]], and the [[Moongrave]] Archlord{{!}}Archlords, during which [[Mercy]] briefly loses control of her power to strike her mother. Ultimately, [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] use a coordinated assault to force [[Malice]] into a retreat, allowing them to claim the unconscious [[Mercy]] and depart despite the opposition of the city's leaders.}}
* '''Chapter 24:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|While [[Suriel]] works to restore the world of [[Vesper]], she and Ozriel lose contact with [[Sector Thirty]] command, leading them to prepare for a likely fatal confrontation with [[the Mad King]]. Simultaneously, [[Makiel]] observes this battle from afar while [[Akura Malice]] spreads news across [[Cradle]] that [[Lindon]] has been corrupted and rebranded as the '[[Empty Ghost]],' a new [[Dreadgod]]. Meanwhile, the [[Oracle Sage]] prepares her forces to strike [[House Arelius]], and [[Lindon]] retreats toward the [[Trackless Sea]] to prepare for his impending battle with the [[Weeping Dragon]].}}

== Characters ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Lindon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Dross]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Malice]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Silent King]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Yerin]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Ziel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Mercy]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Eithan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Emriss Silentborn]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Red Faith]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Northstrider]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Little Blue]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Orthos]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Blood Sage]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Ozriel (Cradle)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Wandering Titan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Charity]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Kelsa]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Sage of Red Faith]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Ozmanthus Arelius]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Suriel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Jai Long]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Reigan Shen]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Emala]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Subject One]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Void Sage]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Jai Chen]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Titan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Weeping Dragon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Pride]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Rune Queen]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Makiel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Ekeri]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Aunt Charity]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Mad King]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Ruby]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Archlady]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Alden Zaius]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Akura Malice]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Winter Sage]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Noroloth]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Helethshan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Kahn Mala]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Seisha]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Sha Miara]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Akura Justice]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Suu]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Daruman]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Fingerling]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Burning Swan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Terkell]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Gerravon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Colossal Moon-eater]]}}

== Locations & groups ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Moongrave]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Redmoon Hall]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Monarchs]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Redmoon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Abidan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Serpent's Grave]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[labyrinth]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Windfall]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Shatterspine Castle]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Sacred Valley]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Akura clan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Everwood]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Black dragons]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Dreadnought City]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[sacred artists]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Blackflame Empire]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Twin Star Sect]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Ashwind continent]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Judges]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Cradle]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[The Eight-Man Empire]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Ancestor's Tomb]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Vroshir]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Everwood continent]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[House Shen]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Rosegold]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Amorenthus]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Sha family]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Golds]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Hall of Judgment]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Sector Seven]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Ashwind]]}}

== Abilities & sacred arts ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[hunger madra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[dream aura]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Hollow Domain]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|dream madra}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Grand Oath Array]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Ruler technique]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Striker technique]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Enforcer technique]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Blood aura]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Heart of Twin Stars]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Empty Palm]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[blood madra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Path of Black Flame]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Path of Last Oath]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Blackflame madra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[black dragon's breath]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[shadow madra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[force madra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[destruction madra]]}}

== Artifacts & constructs ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Moonlight Bridge]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[dream tablets]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[void key]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[The Book of Eternal Night]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Thousand-Mile Cloud]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[gatestone]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|hunger spears}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Remnant]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|launcher construct}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Soulforge]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Silent King’s crown]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Redmoon's Remnant]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[The Fleshripper]]}}

== Events & concepts ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Dreadgod]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Dreadgods]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Archlord]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Bleeding Phoenix]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Void Icon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Monarch]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Herald]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Phoenix]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Blood Shadow]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Blackflame]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[The Void (Cradle)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Way]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Fate]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Uncrowned King Tournament]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Underlord]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Dread War]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|soul-oath}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Sage]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|force aura}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Genesis]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Path of the White Fox]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[pure madra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[The Way]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[soulspace]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Dreamway]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Overlady]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|cloud fortress}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Slumbering Wraith]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Herald Remnant]]}}

== Sources ==
* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cradle
* https://openlibrary.org/works/OL28158998W

{{Willverse books nav}}

[[Category:Books]]
[[Category:Dreadgod]]
